name: Do I need a permit for siding replacement in Pacifica, Daly City, or San Bruno?

mainEntity.name: When does exterior siding replacement require a permit in these cities?
mainEntity.text: When does exterior siding replacement require a permit in these cities?

acceptedAnswer.text: Permit requirements usually depend on the scope of work, not just the siding material. A straightforward like-for-like replacement may be simpler than a project that adds insulation, replaces damaged sheathing, changes wall assemblies, or alters exterior features. In many Bay Area cities, siding jobs that touch structural repairs, fire-resistance upgrades, or significant envelope changes are more likely to need permits and inspections. Because Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, and Half Moon Bay can each apply local rules differently, the safest approach is to have the contractor verify permit needs before demolition begins. This matters because hidden rot often turns a “simple siding job” into a repair project. A good proposal should state whether permits are included, who pulls them, and whether inspection delays are built into the schedule.
acceptedAnswer.description: Many siding replacement projects require a permit when they involve structural repair, sheathing replacement, insulation changes, or exterior alterations. Simple like-for-like cosmetic work may be treated differently, but local rules vary by city and by scope of work, so the contractor should confirm requirements before starting.
